description_long: This object consists of two main boxy units, one larger and gray with a metallic finish, and the other smaller and darker, both featuring vented panels and robust construction. The larger unit has a coiled black cord attached to a classic telephone-style handset, suggesting it is a communication device, possibly a radio transceiver or intercom system. The front panel of the larger unit is equipped with several control knobs, switches, and ports, as well as a speaker grille and indicator lights. The smaller unit appears to be an external speaker or amplifier, with visible venting and a single control or connector on its side. Both units have visible screws and mounting points, indicating they are designed for secure placement in a fixed location. The overall design is utilitarian, with a focus on durability and functionality, suitable for use in garages, workshops, or utility rooms.
description: A rugged, dual-unit electronic communication device with a handset, control knobs, and vented metal casings, likely used for radio or intercom purposes.
description_view_0: Front view showing the control panel, knobs, ports, and attached handset of the main unit, with the smaller unit positioned behind.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the vented panels and mounting screws of both units, with the coiled handset cord visible.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the side ports and connectors of the main unit and the rear of the smaller unit.
description_view_3: Angled rear view focusing on the vented surfaces and the robust, industrial design of both units.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
